# Transcrição: 3. Outbound Referral Call

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Another type of outbound call would be the outbound referral call. Every salesperson should be making referral calls but few actually do. And in fact, let me just back that up a second. Very few salespeople even ask for a referral and I'm going to tell you why. I know because I did this in my own career. In the very beginning of my sales career I was asking for referrals. You know the first week, first month, first couple months I'm asking who do you know, who do you know, who do you know, run around everywhere, who do you know. And the next thing you know people started actually giving me referrals. It was amazing actually. I was like wow, people know people that want my product. And then I ended up with all these leads and didn't know how to call them. So what's the next thing I'm going to do? I'm going to quit asking for referrals because I don't know how to call them. Right? And that's probably happened to you. Just ask yourself, when's the last time you asked for a referral? When's the last time you asked someone for a referral? It's probably been a long time ago and then think back to the time when you had all these leads and you're like I'm not calling them, I can't call them, I don't know how to call them. And then what followed after that was you just quit asking for referrals. We're going to fix that right here. Okay? Look, 91% of customers say they would give you a referral if you would just ask. And that's people that bought from you. What percentage of people do you think would give you a referral that didn't buy from you? In fact I'll tell you this. More people that don't buy from you will give you a referral than people that did buy from you. Okay? I have people that don't buy from me that will give me a lead easier than people that bought from me. 11% of salespeople, only 11% of salespeople ever asked someone for a lead. Imagine that. You sell 100 people a product and only 11% of the time does the salesperson ask for a lead and I think that that would be a big number. Now before we talk about this call, here are the two biggest mistakes you could possibly make when trying to get referrals. Number one, you don't ever even ask. And I know this has happened to you. I guarantee you 99.9% of the people watching this right now, something happened to you over time and you quit asking for leads. I don't know. Maybe your mom or your dad told you, oh, don't ever try to do business with family. My family told me that. I'm like, I'm gonna call Uncle Jay about a car, right? I'm gonna call Uncle Jay about training. I'm gonna call my Uncle Jay about real estate. I've sold all three of those things. I was selling clothes. I'm gonna call my Uncle Jay about clothes every time I do it, my mom would be like, I got in a network marketing company. I'm gonna call my uncle about, don't call family, don't talk to family, don't mix family and business. See, you come up with reasons not to ask for referrals.
